name: Mousse of Duck Liver
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the flavored aspic according to the recipe instructions.
2. Cover the bottom of a pate mold with 1 cup of flavored aspic and refrigerate until hardened (about 15-20 minutes).
3. In a large sauté pan, melt butter over medium heat. Add thyme and shallots; cook for 2 minutes.
4. Add duck liver to the pan and cook until done rare. Season with salt and pepper.
5. Stir in 1 cup port wine and let it sizzle briefly.
6. Transfer the liver mixture into a food processor bowl.
7. In a sauté pan, heat heavy cream over medium heat and add gelatin leaves; stir until dissolved.
8. Pour the hot cream mixture into the food processor with the liver mixture.
9. Puree the mixture in the food processor for about 1 minute.
10. Strain the pureed mixture through a sieve into a large bowl, whisking to combine.
11. Add cognac, lemon juice, salt, pepper, and cayenne; mix thoroughly.
12. Pour the mixture back into the mold, cover with remaining flavored aspic, and refrigerate until set (about 15 minutes).
13. Unmold, slice, and serve on cold plates with toasted bread.
See recipe for Aspic Jelly. (Note: Flavored aspic can be made from scratch or using Knorr Swiss aspic flavored with Port and Cognac.)
